deeperExplanation: Python supports Multiple Inheritance (`class Child(ParentA, ParentB)`). CPython resolves method lookups using the Method Resolution Order (MRO) algorithm (C3 Linearization). Check MRO with `Class.mro()`.
internalImplementation: C3 linearization calculates deterministic MRO order.

examples.code:
class A: pass
class B(A): pass
print(B.mro())  # [<class 'B'>, <class 'A'>, <class 'object'>]

performanceConsiderations.timeComplexity: MRO resolution cached in class structure O(1).

debuggingWalkthrough.bugDescription: Uninitialized parent attributes due to missing super().__init__().

bestPractices: Use composition over inheritance when relationships are not strict 'is-a'.
